programme.desc: News of the September 11 terrorist attacks reaches Luton, and it is not long before airport supervisor Leo Jones is besieged by anxious passengers seeking further information. programme.title: The Bill

When an academic is found dead after a CND rally, Gently and Bacchus are dispatched to Durham University to investigate his background - and find themselves in the middle of a wave of social and sexual rebellion. Drama, starring Martin Shaw and Lee Ingleby, with Sarah Lancashire and Warren Clarke(n)

As Sandra attempts to unravel secrets stretching back over three decades and link what has now become three murders, she still has doubts about the innocence of charming entrepreneur Harry Truman - but that doesn't stop her joining him for lunch on his luxury yacht. Conclusion of a two-part story, with regulars Amanda Redman, Alun Armstrong, Dennis Waterman and Denis Lawson joined by Vincent Regan and George Irving(n)

programme.title: Silent Witness

However, they soon discover there is more at stake than identifying a soldier's remains as the crime scene is preventing the completion of a charity-run sanitation plant, putting thousands of lives at risk from contaminated water. programme.title: Memento (2000)

Unable to remember events for more than a few minutes, he is determined to track down the killer and relies on copious note-taking and reminders tattooed on his body to aid his mission. programme.desc: The escaped zoo animals discover a plane and, following a swift repair job by the penguins, manage to escape the desolate shores of Madagascar. However, they crash-land on the plains of Africa and find themselves face-to-face with their wild counterparts for the first time. Animated comedy sequel, with the voices of Ben Stiller, Chris Rock, David Schwimmer and Jada Pinkett Smith(n)

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at life in North America, which is defined by extreme weather and seasonal change - from tornados that roar across the prairies, to arctic air sweeping through the humid, southern swamps. In winter, lynx prowl the snowy Yukon for snowshoe hares, while Florida manatees seek hot springs to escape the freeze, while in summer fireflies light up the forests at night. Plus, footage of roadrunners cruising the spectacular deserts of Arizona, and polar bears leaping from rocks to hunt beluga whales(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team are in Wiltshire where John Craven is in a wood that's ravaged with ash dieback, and Anita Rani meets a craftsman who's keeping the ancient art of bee skep making alive. Steve Brown pays a visit to one of the country's top wood engravers, and Adam Henson is in Lincolnshire seeing for himself the impact of the recent devastating floods(n)

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at the fauna of Africa, home to the greatest wildlife gatherings on Earth. But even in this land of plenty, animals face huge challenges. At its heart is a vast tropical rainforest full of life, where young chimpanzees learn how to use tools to make the most of the jungles riches. With knowledge passed down from generation to generation, they can access the best forest foods(n)

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing

programme.title: Elizabeth Is Missing
programme.desc: When 80-year-old Maud's best friend Elizabeth fails to turn up for a shopping trip, she is certain that something terrible has happened. Despite living with dementia, she decides to turn detective and sets about trying to solve the mystery, but with her condition it's not always easy to hold on to the clues. Drama, starring Glenda Jackson(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by studio guests to review the day's Premier League action, featuring Aston Villa v Leicester City at Villa Park, Newcastle United v Southampton at St James' Park, Norwich City v Sheffield United at Carrow Road and Brighton & Hove Albion v Wolverhampton Wanderers at Amex Stadium. Plus, all the goals from yesterday's top-flight matches(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Jeremy Dein and Sasha Wass investigate the poisoning of a wealthy female tenant, who was killed by her landlord in 1911, just after signing over her assets to him(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The restorers get to work on a Victorian high chair, a 200-year-old telescope used by a smuggler and a tippling stick thought to have belonged to King George V(n)

programme.title: Love in the Countryside
programme.desc: Sheepdog trainer Katy invites three potential partners to experience a taste of country life in the Cumbrian Fells. While the weather is bleak, painter and decorator John, motorbike champion David and former footballer Carl take to herding sheep and mucking out kennels with remarkable success. Grace takes a day off from her family's dairy to visit a hobby farmer on his own turf, while Sara Cox pays a visit to Cumbrian farmer David to deliver letters from six candidates for a speed dating session(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond(n)

programme.title: Live Snooker: UK Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the 10th day at the Barbican Centre in York, featuring the opening quarter-finals of the prestigious ranking tournament, played over the best of 11 frames. Surprise package Tom Ford's run continued at this stage last year when he overcame Joe Perry 6-2. Hazel Irvine presents, with commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, John Parrott, Ken Doherty, Stephen Hendry, Alan McManus, John Virgo and Dennis Taylor(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: People are invited to try to make money out of their antiques by taking a risk at auction(n)

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Zoe Ball and Rylan Clark-Neal present analysis of the pro-celebrity couples' progress. Celebrity super fans are in the studio to discuss all things ballroom(n)

programme.title: Live Snooker: UK Championship
programme.desc: Further coverage of the 10th day at the Barbican Centre in York, featuring the concluding quarter-finals of the prestigious ranking tournament, played over the best of 11 frames. Ronnie O'Sullivan recorded another dominant victory at this stage last year, beating Martin O'Donnell 6-1. Hazel Irvine presents, with commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, John Parrott, Ken Doherty, Stephen Hendry, Alan McManus, John Virgo and Dennis Taylor(n)

programme.title: Mountain Vets
programme.desc: On the road, senior vet Rachael Frew heads to a dairy farm to see a cow with a suspected displaced stomach. Recognising it as a life-threatening condition, she decides to operate immediately and tries to move the stomach back into place by hand. However, it proves a tricky process and the farmer is called upon to assist. Meanwhile, specialist small animal surgeon Martin Fitzpatrick operates on young sheepdog Ellie in a bid to save her leg, which has been badly injured by some wire. Narrated by Kevin Murphy(n)

programme.title: The Name of the Rose
programme.desc: Salvatore is returned to the abbey with the Occitan girl, who is accused of witchcraft by Bernard Gui. Salvatore is tortured and confesses to Bernardo that he witnessed Remigio kill a bishop when they were both followers of the heretic Dolcino. Remigio at last confesses to being a follower of Dolcino and of still believing in the doctrine of poverty. William and the Franciscans are alarmed at the confession and of Bernard Gui linking the heretical teachings of Dolcino to their own vows of poverty. John Turturro, Rupert Everett and Greta Scarano star(n)

programme.title: Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond the Lobby
programme.desc: Giles Coren and Monica Galetti travel to the Anantara Al Jabal Al Akhdar in Oman, the highest five-star hotel in the Middle East and a resort seeking to blend local tradition with modern tastes. Built to resemble an ancient Omani fort, and situated 2,000 metres above sea level on the rim of a dramatic canyon, the hotel has recently made the most of its location by constructing a jaw-dropping via ferrata and zip-wire rope course along the cliff edge(n)

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain looks back on the latest events in news and politics, with the help of Tom Allen, Ed Gamble, Kerry Godliman, Rhys James and Rosie Jones and regular panellist Hugh Dennis. As usual, stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on one another(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events. Presented by Kirsty Wark(n)

programme.title: Snooker: UK Championship
programme.desc: Seema Jaswal presents action from this evening's quarter-finals on day 10 at the Barbican Centre in York(n)

programme.title: Calvary (2014)
programme.desc: A priest in a rural Irish village receives a confession from a man who vows to kill him in one week as punishment for the Catholic Church's history of child abuse. The cleric spends his last seven days trying to put his community's affairs in order, but finds himself surrounded by people who harbour resentments of their own against the Church. Drama, starring Brendan Gleeson, Chris O'Dowd, Kelly Reilly, Aidan Gillen and Dylan Moran(n)

programme.title: Ask the Leaders - Wales
programme.desc: Leader of Plaid Cymru Adam Price takes questions from a studio audience about the party's policy platform for the general election. Presented by Bethan Rhys Roberts(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: As Prince Andrew steps back from his public duties, Darragh MacIntyre hears from the victims of is former friend and convicted child sex offender Jeffrey Epstein. The Duke of York has been widely criticised for not expressing sympathy for these women during his television interview with the BBC's Newsnight programme. Virginia Roberts Giuffre claims she was trafficked by Epstein and forced to have sex with the prince, something he denies. In her first interview on British TV, she tells her story and reveals new details about her time with Epstein. Reporter Darragh MacIntyre also looks at another of the duke's friends, Ghislaine Maxwell, and investigates her alleged role in Epstein's prolific sex offending. As the FBI investigation continues, Panorama asks what next in the scandal of Epstein and the prince?(n)

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at life in South America, where animals must specialise if they are to carve out a niche. In Patagonia, a puma mother draws on a lifetime's experience to catch prey three times her weight, while rarely seen Andean bears clamber 30 metres into the cloud forest canopy to find elusive fruit. Poison dart frogs use ingenious methods to keep their tadpoles safe, anacondas stalk capuchin monkeys, and swifts make death-defying flights through Igauzu Falls on the border between Argentina and Brazil(n)

programme.title: Novels That Shaped Our World
programme.desc: The second documentary examining fictional writing from three different perspectives considers the subject of race and colonialism. It looks at books including Robinson Crusoe, the first novel ever published in English, in 1719, whose hero was a slave trader, and two famous American novels published more than a century apart - Harriet Beecher Stowe's Uncle Tom's Cabin and Toni Morrison's Beloved. The programme also comes up to date with titles such as Malorie Blackman's Noughts and Crosses and Paul Beatty's The Sell Out(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Naomi Wilkinson and Tim Warwood discover some uninvited animal guests, including a herd of elephants in a hotel, and an ant that indulges in some shocking behaviour(n)

programme.title: Top Class
programme.desc: Two teams take on the challenge of answering questions(n)

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Christmas Cards and a Festive Badge Giveaway. With Christmas fast approaching, see how to make this year's Blue Peter Christmas card(n)

programme.title: The Amelia Gething Complex
programme.desc: Amelia sets Vinny up on a series of strange dates. Wallace gets engaged to a cake(n)

programme.title: The Amelia Gething Complex
programme.desc: Wallace thinks he has found the world's last dragon egg(n)

programme.title: Tinker Bell and the Secret of the Wings (2012)
programme.desc: The fairy becomes curious about the mysterious winter forest where fairies never dare to go. She travels into the cold woods, where she meets a new friend and uncovers a threat to both their homes that they must work together to prevent. Disney animated adventure, with the voices of Mae Whitman, Lucy Hale, Anjelica Huston and Timothy Dalton(n)

programme.title: Deadly 60 on a Mission
programme.desc: Steve Backshall and the team search for lethal beasts in Venezuela. In the wetlands, he heads into crocodile-infested waters for a shocking encounter with an electric eel, and joins a team trying to save the endangered Orinoco crocodile. He then journeys to the rainforest, where a dark cave offers up the scolopendra - a giant centipede armed with a powerful venomous bite - and finally he crosses the border into Brazil for a close-up view of the maned wolf, one of the world's rarest canines(n)

programme.title: Robot Wars
programme.desc: Dara O Briain and Angela Scanlon present the second heat, as eight teams battle it out to win a single place in the grand final, pitting robots Cherub, PP3D, Draven, Push to Exit, Hobgoblin, Behemoth, Cobra and Eruption against each other in the arena(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Gemma Oaten and Samantha Renke hunt for antiques in Lancashire, accompanied by experts Philip Serrell and James Braxton. They discover the history of Blackpool's tram network and Lancaster's Grand Theatre, while a donkey makes a surprise appearance at the auction in Kendal(n)

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace hosts the first round of Knockout week, as the 12 chefs to make it through the finals compete to stay in the contest. The chefs face an invention test in which they must not use any modern kitchen gadgets. The four weakest performing contestants then go through to a cook-off, to decide which two will be eliminated from the competition(n)

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: It's Knockout Week, and with only ten chefs remaining in the competition, the first group of hopefuls are set two daunting challenges by Monica and Marcus. In the brand-new Pop Up Challenge, the chefs have to show off their versatility and style, creating and serving a dish that will win over the judges and the specially-invited guests. Back in the MasterChef kitchen, the chefs are pitted against each other in a cook-off to secure a place in the Semi-Finals(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A retrospective look at the television appearances made over the years by Days of Wine and Roses actor Jack Lemmon, capturing the highlights and milestones of his life and career. Narrated by actress Sylvia Syms(n)

programme.title: Fire Down Below (1957)
programme.desc: Felix and Tony run a tramp steamer together in the West Indies, but their seemingly unshakeable friendship is tossed aside when they both fall in love with the same woman. Tony tries to undermine his rival by exposing his illegal activities, while Felix responds with deadly intent. Romantic drama, with Rita Hayworth, Robert Mitchum and Jack Lemmon(n)

programme.title: Live Snooker: UK Championship
programme.desc: Hazel Irvine presents concluding coverage of the opening semi-final on the 11th day of the prestigious ranking tournament at the Barbican Centre in York, played over the best of 11 frames. At the corresponding stage last year, Ronnie O'Sullivan recorded a 6-1 victory over Tom Ford to progress to the final. With commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, John Parrott, Ken Doherty, Stephen Hendry, Alan McManus and Dennis Taylor(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Former winners Rick Edwards, Scarlett Moffatt, Naga Munchetty and David O'Doherty test their general knowledge skills on this week's special House of Games: Champion editions(n)

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: On the North coast of Kent, new RNLI crew member Ruth is on board for one of her first shouts - a member of the public has spotted what looks like an upturned boat out at sea. Off the Essex coast, the crew of West Mersea station launch to reports of a life raft in the North Sea. And at Clacton, the crew get a call in the middle of the night to look for two people in the sea(n)

programme.title: Wales: Land of the Wild
programme.desc: The documentary peeks into the lives of several animals throughout autumn, the season of change, when about half of all British breeding goshawks come to Wales, thriving in woodlands across the country. On a smaller scale, the ivy bee synchronises its six-week life cycle around the plant from which it takes its name, while on an island off the Pembrokeshire coast, grey seals and their pups relax in the late season sunshine. But before long winter sets in and flocks of starlings find a place to roost, heading to the coast where temperatures are milder(n)

programme.title: Live Snooker: UK Championship
programme.desc: Hazel Irvine presents coverage of the second semi-final on the 11th and penultimate day of the prestigious ranking tournament at the Barbican Centre in York, played over the best of 11 frames, as the line-up for the showpiece match is confirmed. Mark Allen claimed a hard-fought 6-5 triumph over Stuart Bingham at the corresponding stage of last year's competition to reach the final for the second time in his career. However, like in 2011 when he was defeated by Judd Trump, 'The Pistol' fell short at the final hurdle once again, losing out to Ronnie O'Sullivan. With commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, John Parrott, Ken Doherty, Stephen Hendry, Alan McManus and Dennis Taylor(n)

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig delves into the topic of Questions and Qualifications, with regular panellist Alan Davies and guest players Ade Adepitan, Nish Kumar and Holly Walsh. As usual, the questions are so difficult points are instead awarded for the answers the host finds most interesting as opposed to correct(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Sarah Millican hosts the fourth annual Christmas special, recorded before a packed house at London's Hammersmith Apollo. Taking to the stage for tonight's festive line-up are Ahir Shah and Laura Lexx, two performers making their Live at the Apollo debuts following successful shows at this year's Edinburgh Fringe, while the bill is completed by Gary Delaney(n)

programme.title: Trumbo (2015)
programme.desc: Biopic of the acclaimed Hollywood screenwriter, who was jailed and blacklisted in the 1940s for his left-wing political views. On his release from prison, he manages to continue writing using a series of pseudonyms, and his newfound success leads many to question the validity of the blacklist. Biopic of the writer, starring Bryan Cranston, Diane Lane and Helen Mirren(n)

programme.title: Short Term 12 (2013)
programme.desc: A counsellor at a foster care centre for troubled teenagers tries to help a gifted newcomer realise her talents and assists a 17-year-old in dealing with the prospect of having to leave the facility when he turns 18. However, helping them get a good start in life forces her to come to terms with her own difficult past. Drama, starring Brie Larson and John Gallagher Jr(n)

programme.title: The Name of the Rose
programme.desc: Salvatore is returned to the abbey with the Occitan girl, who is accused of witchcraft by Bernard Gui. Salvatore is tortured and confesses to Bernardo that he witnessed Remigio kill a bishop when they were both followers of the heretic Dolcino. Remigio at last confesses to being a follower of Dolcino and of still believing in the doctrine of poverty. William and the Franciscans are alarmed at the confession and of Bernard Gui linking the heretical teachings of Dolcino to their own vows of poverty. John Turturro, Rupert Everett and Greta Scarano star(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: The Instant Gardener
programme.desc: Helen Skelton and expert designer Danny Clarke head to Northamptonshire to help create a low-allergen garden for a couple whose young son is affected by severe allergies(n)

programme.title: Around the World in 80 Gardens
programme.desc: Monty Don journeys to the Mediterranean, the cradle of European civilisation, for the next stage in his horticultural tour. In Italy, he witnesses the elaborate stone and water gardens created during the high Renaissance. He also investigates the influence of Moorish culture on European gardens, visiting the Aguedal in Marrakesh, and Spain's magnificent Alhambra(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and Helen Skelton explore the Tees Valley, while Tom Heap investigates whether enough is being done to protect UK farming and food production from flooding(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen(n)

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: In the last programme of knockout week, the second group of five face two tough tests. First they are given three hours, with limited equipment and facilities, to produce a knockout plate that showcases their versatility and style. This dish is judged by a group of diners specialising in the street food and pop-up industry - and the contestant that wins the most votes sails through to the semi-finals. The other four are left to produce a showstopper dish in just 90 minutes - hoping it will be enough to get them through(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's Secret France
programme.desc: Rick's journey takes him to the Spanish border and the Mediterranean coast. He discovers how Catalan and French influences have shaped the culinary legacy of Roussilon in the foothills of the Pyrenees, and samples some of the area's famous mountain cuisine, including grilled sausages, snails and fresh fish caught in a self-contained seawater lake - all with generous servings of the region's famous garlic mayonnaise. Back in his country kitchen, Rick prepares pork and beef meatballs in a tomato and piment sauce(n)

programme.title: Live Snooker: UK Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the final of the prestigious ranking tournament at the Barbican Centre in York, played over the best of 19 frames, with a maximum of eight to be played in this session. Ronnie O'Sullivan dominated the opening session of last year's showpiece match, taking a 6-2 lead over Mark Allen. Presented by Hazel Irvine, with commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, John Parrott, Ken Doherty, Stephen Hendry, Alan McManus, John Virgo and Dennis Taylor(n)

programme.title: Athletics
programme.desc: Action from the annual event, held this year in Lisbon, Portugal. With commentary by Paula Radcliffe and Steve Cram(n)

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace visits a cheese factory in Gateshead, where 3,000 tonnes of spreadable cheese are produced every year. He follows the production of jalapeno chilli flavoured cheese, from a 28,000-litre delivery of milk to 5,400 squeezy tubes. Meanwhile, Cherry Healey finds out how bacteria are responsible for the huge variety in smell, taste and appearance of different types of cheese. She also learns that some smelly cheeses contain the same bacteria that are responsible for stinky feet(n)

programme.title: Live Snooker: UK Championship
programme.desc: Concluding coverage of the final of the prestigious ranking tournament at the Barbican Centre in York, played over the best of 19 frames, with a maximum of 11 to be played in this session. Although he had only made sporadic appearances all season, Ronnie O'Sullivan showed no sign of rust as he retained the title last year, overcoming a valiant second-session display by Mark Allen to prevail 10-6 and become the most successful player in the competition's history ahead of Steve Davis with seven triumphs. The result also gave 'The Rocket' a 19th Triple Crown title, having also won five world titles and The Masters seven times, to put him one ahead of Stephen Hendry at the top of the all-time list, as he further strengthened his claim to being the greatest player of all time. Presented by Hazel Irvine, with commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, John Parrott, Ken Doherty, Stephen Hendry, Alan McManus and Dennis Taylor(n)

programme.title: Defiance (2008)
programme.desc: Three Jewish brothers escape into the woods during the 1939 Nazi invasion of Poland, and launch a series of guerrilla attacks on German forces. As word of their actions spreads, other refugees join them to form a larger resistance movement. However, one of the brothers fears their struggle is doomed. Second World War drama, starring Daniel Craig, Liev Schreiber and Jamie Bell(n)

programme.title: World News Today
programme.desc: The day's leading stories(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Bruno Brookes and Mike Read present the edition first aired on December 1, featuring performances by Rick Astley, Michael Jackson, Pet Shop Boys, George Michael, Angry Anderson, Cliff Richard, Phil Collins, Bros, Robin Beck and Humanoid(n)

programme.title: Sound of Song
programme.desc: As his exploration of great songs reaches the modern era, Neil Brand charts how the arrival of digital technology and the computer transformed music. He talks synthesisers with Yes keyboardist Rick Wakeman, discusses sampling with Public Enemy's chief producer Hank Shocklee, uncovers the surprising lo-fi origins of Bruce Springsteen's Born in the USA, and finds out how Cher changed her voice on the smash hit Believe(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell and Caron Keating present the edition first aired on December 8 featuring performances by Status Quo, Angry Anderson, Phil Collins, Petula Clark, New Order, Inner City, Erasure, Kylie Minogue and Jason Donovan, Cliff Richard and Bananarama(n)

programme.title: Country Music by Ken Burns
programme.desc: The history of the art form and how it evolved between 1964 and 1968. During a time of cultural upheaval, country music reflected a changing America, as Loretta Lynn addresseed women's concerns, Merle Haggard championed the common man, and audiences looked beyond skin colour to embrace Charley Pride. Meanwhile, Johnny Cash descended into the chaos of drug addiction, though The Man in Black found salvation through the intervention of June Carter and a landmark album that critics still applaud. Narrated by Peter Coyote(n)

programme.title: Country Music by Ken Burns
programme.desc: How, between 1968 and 1972, country music responded to a US nation divided by the Vietnam War. Army captain-turned-songwriter Kris Kristofferson set a new lyrical standard, and artists like Bob Dylan and the Byrds found a recording home in Nashville. Plus, a hippie combo from California, the Nitty Gritty Band, created a landmark album that bridged the gap between the generations. Narrated by Peter Coyote(n)

programme.title: Country Kings at the BBC
programme.desc: A chronological compilation of performances from the BBC archive by male country singers, ranging from the Everly Brothers and Jerry Lee Lewis to Garth Brooks and Willie Nelson. Featuring classic songs from studio shows by Glen Campbell, Charley Pride, George Hamilton IV, Kenny Rogers, Clint Black, Johnny Cash and Eric Church(n)

programme.title: Sings Stevie Wonder
programme.desc: A trawl through the BBC archives, unearthing 50 years of covers of Wonder's classic songbook, featuring artists including Cilla Black, Jimmy Helms, Dionne Warwick, the Osmonds, India Arie and James Morrison. Among the clips is a storming performance of Ed Sheeran with Jools Holland and his Rhythm & Blues Orchestra taking on Master Blaster (Jammin')(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Bruno Brookes and Mike Read present the edition first aired on December 1, featuring performances by Rick Astley, Michael Jackson, Pet Shop Boys, George Michael, Angry Anderson, Cliff Richard, Phil Collins, Bros, Robin Beck and Humanoid(n)

programme.title: Sound of Song
programme.desc: As his exploration of great songs reaches the modern era, Neil Brand charts how the arrival of digital technology and the computer transformed music. He talks synthesisers with Yes keyboardist Rick Wakeman, discusses sampling with Public Enemy's chief producer Hank Shocklee, uncovers the surprising lo-fi origins of Bruce Springsteen's Born in the USA, and finds out how Cher changed her voice on the smash hit Believe(n)

programme.title: Tunes for Tyrants: Music & Power with Suzy Klein
programme.desc: The use of music by totalitarian states in the 1930s, when Adolf Hitler and Josef Stalin manipulated it to suit their own ideologies and composers came under extraordinary pressure(n)

programme.title: All Aboard: The Great Reindeer Migration
programme.desc: Documentary follows Norway's Sami reindeer herds as they migrate across the mountainous region of Finnmark, far north of the Arctic Circle. The journey, a little over 160 miles, takes the herd a week, travelling north from the inland winter feeding grounds to their coastal summer pastures. The film travels with the Sara family and their herd around the clock, as they cross the arctic wilderness to reach the coastal island of Kvaloya Fala in time for the calves to be born(n)

programme.title: The Sinner
programme.desc: Harry is concerned that he has no memory of checking himself into the Rockford Motel, while Benji enters a plea of not guilty at Julian's arraignment. Vera asks Harry to act as a defence witness, causing a rift between him and Heather. Crime drama, starring Bill Pullman, Natalie Paul and Carrie Coon(n)

programme.title: The Sinner
programme.desc: Harry revisits Carmen Bell in the psychiatric institution, but discovers she has been placed in isolation after having a bad reaction to a phone call the day before. Vera tells Harry that Julian is being medicated at the juvenile detention centre. Crime drama, starring Bill Pullman and Carrie Coon(n)

programme.title: From Elton John to Gary Barlow: Celebrating 100 Concerts Live at Eden
programme.desc: A look back over the last 18 years of the Eden Sessions. Introduced by Nile Rodgers, who headlined the 100th show, highlights include Pulp, who headlined the first one in July 2002. Set against the backdrop of the Eden biomes, there are also performances by Duran Duran, Bastille, Muse, Gary Barlow, Van Morrison, Madness, Primal Scream and Lionel Richie(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Bruno Brookes and Mike Read present the edition first aired on December 1, featuring performances by Rick Astley, Michael Jackson, Pet Shop Boys, George Michael, Angry Anderson, Cliff Richard, Phil Collins, Bros, Robin Beck and Humanoid(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell and Caron Keating present the edition first aired on December 8 featuring performances by Status Quo, Angry Anderson, Phil Collins, Petula Clark, New Order, Inner City, Erasure, Kylie Minogue and Jason Donovan, Cliff Richard and Bananarama(n)

programme.title: Woody Guthrie: Three Chords and the Truth
programme.desc: Profile of the legendary American songwriter, who from the 1930s until his death in 1967, wrote hundreds of political, folk and children's songs, along with ballads and improvised works, influencing successive generations of musicians such as Bob Dylan, Bruce Springsteen, Joan Baez and Billy Bragg. This documentary features a previously unheard recording of one of his most famous songs, among a rare and unseen archive(n)

programme.title: Music Moguls: Masters of Pop
programme.desc: A look at the producers who created the signature sounds that have defined key periods in rock and pop history. Contributors include Trevor Horn, who reflects on 'inventing' the sound of the 1980s, Lamont Dozier, who recalls the impact of Motown, and Tony Visconti recollecting David Bowie's song Heroes. Narrated by musician, producer and guitarist Nile Rodgers(n)

programme.title: Nigel Kennedy at the BBC
programme.desc: A compilation of the musician's performances from the BBC archive, documenting the British violinist and violist's development and career, from a seven-year old child on Town and Around, to his virtuoso show-stopper Czardas from the Last Night of the Proms 2013. Featuring interviews with him through the years, the programme studies his versatility of styles, including classical, experimental and jazz(n)

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Reading v Arsenal at Adams Park, Chelsea v Manchester City at Cherry Red Records Stadium and Manchester United v Everton at Leigh Sports Village(n)

programme.title: Canals - The Making of a Nation
programme.desc: Liz McIvor discovers how carving up the landscape in order to build canals helped further man's understanding of the earth below. William Smith, a surveyor on the Somerset Coal Canal, discovered a way of ordering layers of rocks and eventually created the first geological map of England and Wales. His ideas about the age and formation of the earth were developed long before Darwin published his theories of evolution(n)

programme.title: James Galway at the BBC
programme.desc: The flautist, who turned 80 today, reflects on a career spanning more than 40 years. He looks back on TV appearances from Parkinson and Top of the Pops to Wogan and Songs of Praise(n)

programme.title: Ireland with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Two-part programme exploring the history of both the Republic of Ireland and Northern Ireland and examining the rich culture and troubled past. In the first episode, Simon arrives by ferry in Co Wexford and travels along the coast, joining the thousands who try to climb the sacred mountain of Croagh Patrick. He crosses the border to visit Crom Castle and Londonderry, before journeying to the northernmost point at Malin Head(n)

programme.title: The Sky at Night
programme.desc: The presenters look back on the major stories of 2019 - including the New Horizons mission to the most distant world mankind has ever visited, and the first picture of a black hole(n)

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: An edition first shown in July 2012 focusing on Curiosity, Nasa's mission to investigate the possibility of life on Mars. The agency hoped to land a nuclear-powered rover vehicle, which cost $2.5billion to develop, on the surface of the planet by winching it down from a rocket-powered crane, then use it to explore the terrain. The programme features contributions by the project's chief scientist John Grotzinger and discovers the lessons experts learned from previous expeditions, many of which ended in failure(n)

programme.title: Revolutions: The Ideas That Changed the World
programme.desc: Jim Al-Khalili presents a look at the history behind the telescope, from the earliest experiments with optics in eighth-century Baghdad to the Hubble Space Telescope. The programme also reveals how Galileo constructed his own telescope and how a New York property boom led to the discovery of ways of measuring a star's distance from Earth(n)

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: Animated antics with the adventurous aardvark and his friends(n)

programme.title: Dennis & Gnasher Unleashed!

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as famous TV experts take part in the general knowledge quiz, trying to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. Taking part are Mary-Ann Ochota, Adam Hart-Davis, Martin Roberts, Michael Fish, Carrie Grant, David Grant, Charlie Ross and Catherine Southon(n)

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present a music-based quiz show in which contestants must name as many hit songs and artists as possible under intense pressure, with a £10,000 jackpot up for grabs. This week's contestants are couple Alana and Scott from Alexandria in Scotland, brother and sister Devon and Davina from London and Mancunian sisters Sy and Seleena, whose dad used to be a popstar(n)

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman host the semi-final, in which Emma Barton, Kelvin Fletcher, Chris Ramsey and Karim Zeroual find themselves one step closer to hoisting the Glitterball trophy aloft. As the competition is in its final stages, each couple will now perform not one but two routines in a bid to impress the judges and audience at home. Shirley Ballas, Bruno Tonioli, Craig Revel Horwood and Motsi Mabuse will be on hand to give their penultimate judgement(n)

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's Big Show
programme.desc: The lively comedian hosts an evening of entertainment, recorded in front of an audience at the London Palladium. Joining Michael this week are global superstar Camila Cabello, who will be singing her new single Easy, and former King of the Jungle and football legend Harry Redknapp, who hands over his phone to play Send to All. Michael also wakes up one of the biggest boy bands in the world for an extra special Midnight Gameshow with Westlife, plus there are incredible surprises in store for members of the audience(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Charlie learns that his friends are always there for him as he struggles to come to terms with Duffy's advancing illness, and the realisation that he can't afford to pay for her long-term care. Archie saves a hungover Will from partnering Dylan on his shift and the pair get to work on treating a guest patient, while Rash feels isolated and left behind as Mason flies ever higher(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Manchester City v Manchester United at the Etihad Stadium and Everton v Chelsea at Goodison Park. Pep Guardiola's side won both meetings with their fierce rivals last season, including a 3-1 victory on this ground in November 2018, with David Silva, Sergio Aguero and Ilkay Gundogan scoring the goals. Plus, action from Bournemouth v Liverpool at the Vitality Stadium, Tottenham Hotspur v Burnley at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium, and Watford v Crystal Palace at Vicarage Road(n)

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by former players Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present highlights of Thursday night's game - Chicago Bears v Dallas Cowboys at Soldier Field(n)

programme.title: Memento (2000)
programme.desc: An insurance investigator suffers from short-term memory loss after an attack in which his wife was also raped and murdered. Unable to remember events for more than a few minutes, he is determined to track down the killer and relies on copious note-taking and reminders tattooed on his body to aid his mission. Christopher Nolan's thriller, starring Guy Pearce, Carrie-Anne Moss, Joe Pantoliano and Mark Boone Jr(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Chris Mason and Babita Sharma present a round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Manchester City v Manchester United at the Etihad Stadium and Everton v Chelsea at Goodison Park. Pep Guardiola's side won both meetings with their fierce rivals last season, including a 3-1 victory on this ground in November 2018, with David Silva, Sergio Aguero and Ilkay Gundogan scoring the goals. Plus, action from Bournemouth v Liverpool at the Vitality Stadium, Tottenham Hotspur v Burnley at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium, and Watford v Crystal Palace at Vicarage Road(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00(n)

programme.title: Sunday Politics Wales
programme.desc: The latest political news and debate, and reports from around Wales(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Seeing her daughter cope with cerebral palsy has inspired Bradford mum Nicola that a new life in Perth, Australia is the way forward. Nicki Chapman follows the family's progress(n)

programme.title: X-Ray
programme.desc: Rachel Treadaway-Williams meets the residents of an apartment block whose luxury flats have a number of problems, while Omar Hamdi hears from the owner of a mobility scooter that has broken down six times in one year. Lucy Owen finds out why some Christmas gifts and costumes for pets might not be a good idea(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Thomas Plant guide two teams scouring the Royal Welsh Showground in Builth Wells for bargains to take to auction. Presenter Charlie Ross monitors their progress and also finds out about a weird and wonderful collection of bicycles(n)

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Claire McCollum is at Beamish open-air museum in Co Durham to find out how Edwardians celebrated Christmas. Plus, music for Advent, with Rick Astley performing When a Child Is Born(n)

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Ben Fogle and Kate Humble go behind the scenes at Longleat House and Safari Park(n)

programme.title: Madagascar: Escape 2 Africa (2008)
programme.desc: The escaped zoo animals discover a plane and, following a swift repair job by the penguins, manage to escape the desolate shores of Madagascar. However, they crash-land on the plains of Africa and find themselves face-to-face with their wild counterparts for the first time. Animated comedy sequel, with the voices of Ben Stiller, Chris Rock, David Schwimmer and Jada Pinkett Smith(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Great Britain sprinter Desiree Henry, Australia cricketer Mel Jones, netball star Serena Guthrie and former England goalkeeper Paul Robinson join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: Inside the Supermarket
programme.desc: Sainsbury's embarks on an ambitious makeover of its store in Hedge End, Southampton, with manager Becky and her team eager not to lose precious custom while the refurbishment takes place. At head office, two new food brands - a chorizo snack and a fresh fruit tea - are being trialled across 70 stores, while in retail, plans are under way for this year's Christmas campaign(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team are in Wiltshire where John Craven is in a wood that's ravaged with ash dieback, and Anita Rani meets a craftsman who's keeping the ancient art of bee skep making alive. Steve Brown pays a visit to one of the country's top wood engravers, and Adam Henson is in Lincolnshire seeing for himself the impact of the recent devastating floods(n)

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at the fauna of Africa, home to the greatest wildlife gatherings on Earth. But even in this land of plenty, animals face huge challenges. At its heart is a vast tropical rainforest full of life, where young chimpanzees learn how to use tools to make the most of the jungles riches. With knowledge passed down from generation to generation, they can access the best forest foods(n)

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman present the last results show, which will decide who will be going through to next weekend's live final. The scores awarded by judges Shirley Ballas, Motsi Mabuse, Bruno Tonioli and Craig Revel Horwood are combined with the viewers' votes, and the two lowest scoring couples must compete in a dance-off to retain their place in the contest. With music by Niall Horan and a performance by ballet dancer Carlos Acosta(n)

programme.title: His Dark Materials
programme.desc: Lyra discovers the horrific truth behind the Gobblers' activities in the North. She must now use all her wits to help free those around her and avoid suffering a terrible fate. Adaptation of Philip Pullman's fantasy adventure novel series, starring Dafne Keen(n)

programme.title: Elizabeth Is Missing
programme.desc: When 80-year-old Maud's best friend Elizabeth fails to turn up for a shopping trip, she is certain that something terrible has happened. Despite living with dementia, she decides to turn detective and sets about trying to solve the mystery, but with her condition it's not always easy to hold on to the clues. Drama, starring Glenda Jackson(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by studio guests to review the day's Premier League action, featuring Aston Villa v Leicester City at Villa Park, Newcastle United v Southampton at St James' Park, Norwich City v Sheffield United at Carrow Road and Brighton & Hove Albion v Wolverhampton Wanderers at Amex Stadium. Plus, all the goals from yesterday's top-flight matches(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein revisit 1926 to explore a case of Leeds landlady Lily Waterhouse who died, it was believed, at the hands of her lodger Louie Calvert, who had only been living at the house for a matter of weeks(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and Martell Maxwell explore properties about to be auctioned in Tonypandy in the Rhondda Valley, Chesterfield, and Burnley(n)

programme.title: Fake Britain
programme.desc: Dom Littlewood and Michelle Ackerley report on the fake cash machine scam used by criminals to steal millions of pounds, the bogus medical devices that could prove deadly and the counterfeit wireless phone chargers that can set a house on fire. Plus, Michelle tests fake airpods and reveals the harm they can cause(n)

programme.title: A1: Britain's Longest Road
programme.desc: Police are alerted to an emergency after a car collides with a huge 36-ton crane. Plus, A1 traffic cops battle to clear two dangerous vehicle breakdowns(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning hosts, as experts Thomas Forrester and Stephanie Connell help two teams find items of value at Nottingham racecourse to take to auction. Anita also visits the Museum of Timekeeping, near Newark, to find out about a clock that kept millions punctual(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Zara worries about Joe's state of mind, Karen has a problem with speed, while Bear and Jimmi are introduced. A beer connoisseur travels to taste the Clash of the Icons, but the beer is missing(n)

programme.title: The Mallorca Files
programme.desc: Ines sends detectives Max and Miranda to the home of famous German reality TV host and record producer Jurgen Kuhl to investigate a report that equipment is being stolen. Max finds he has unwittingly proposed to his long-term girlfriend Carmen, while Miranda is also offered a proposal - one she'd be mad to refuse(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton assists a couple with a love of entertaining who have £450,000 to find a country home in Leicestershire where they can host family and friends. Alistair also meets the Duchess of Rutland, who tells him all about an exciting discovery at Belvoir Castle(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers are delighted when they are asked to come up with a design for a wildlife haven for a young family in West Sussex. Each of the designers is eager to nab the opportunity, but whose plan will homeowners Clare and Paul choose?(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Ceramics expert Kirstin Ramsey prays for divine intervention trying to rebuild a saintly statue. A antique firing toy cannon and a novelty Victorian inkwell in the shape of a hat are also appraised by the team for refurbishment(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: Party Election Broadcast
programme.desc: By the Scottish Labour Party(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances(n)

programme.title: Test Drive
programme.desc: Grado hosts a car-based game show in which three teams from Perth answer questions via a specially-adapted satnav that will determine their route to a well-known tourist attraction. Correct responses mean they will follow the shortest route, but if they are wrong there will be diversions, wrong turns and miles added to their trip(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Louise is rushed to hospital in labour, with Keanu by her side all the way. Back in the Square, Phil confronts Sharon about Lisa's accusations - and also tries to save his reputation - but Ben is the one who hatches a plan to save the Mitchells' business. Mick's idea to win Ollie a school award looks promising, while Jean volunteers at the hospital, where her thoughts turn to Daniel(n)

programme.title: Election 2019
programme.desc: The leaders of the two main political parties - Boris Johnson of the Conservatives and Jeremy Corbyn of Labour - go head to head in a forum chaired in Southampton by Nick Robinson. Following unprecedented turbulance within Westminster over the past three-and-a-half years, the two will hope to explain why their objectives and visions point out the correct path for Britain to take over the next five years. This marks what will probably be the last occasion the two will meet prior to Polling Day on December 12(n)

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Actor Stephen Mangan assumes the role of host, with actor Brian Cox and comedian Roisin Conaty joining regular team leaders Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Dwayne Johnson and Kevin Hart talk about their new movie Jumanji: The Next Level, Jodie Whittaker discusses the new season of Doctor Who and Michael Palin reflects on his latest travel book, North Korea Journal. Plus, music by - and a word from - singer-songwriter Harry Styles(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Great Britain sprinter Desiree Henry, Australia cricketer Mel Jones, netball star Serena Guthrie and former England goalkeeper Paul Robinson join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: One Night: Joshua v Ruiz
programme.desc: A look back at the first bout between Anthony Joshua and Andy Ruiz Jr, which took place on June 1 this year. Both fighters, former boxers and fans who were there on the night, including Sylvester Stallone, recount their experiences of the night, which ended in a stunning upset(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: As the tenth week of the competition begins, Lord sugar summons the candidates to Burlington House in Central London. Here, the remaining candidates are informed that for this week's task, they have been challenged to create and sell their own perfume, coming up with their own signature scent and design to sell to major retailers. For one team a failure to make decisions proves problematic, while on the other team a bold attempt to make their fragrance stand out does not go to plan. Back in the boardroom, one of the candidates is sent home from the competition(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Host Matt Tebbutt is joined by fellow chefs Ruth Hansom and Jose Pizarro, while actor and singer Luke Evans faces his food heaven or hell. Olly Smith chooses the wines(n)

programme.title: Flour Power
programme.desc: Lennox, Fraser, Jamie and Becca- colleagues from the combined companies Avian and Blue2 in Broughty Ferry - compete against each other in a series of baking challenges(n)

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests review the week's headlines and look ahead to forthcoming matches, including the Premier League Manchester derby between City and United at the Etihad Stadium(n)

programme.title: Live Snooker: UK Championship
programme.desc: Hazel Irvine presents coverage of the opening semi-final on the 11th day of the prestigious ranking tournament at the Barbican Centre in York, played over the best of 11 frames. At the corresponding stage last year, eventual champion Ronnie O'Sullivan recorded a 6-1 victory over outsider Tom Ford to progress to the final. With commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, John Parrott, Ken Doherty, Stephen Hendry, Alan McManus and Dennis Taylor(n)

programme.desc: All the results and news from today's matches across the country(n)

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at life in North America, which is defined by extreme weather and seasonal change - from tornados that roar across the prairies, to arctic air sweeping through the humid, southern swamps. In winter, lynx prowl the snowy Yukon for snowshoe hares, while Florida manatees seek hot springs to escape the freeze, while in summer fireflies light up the forests at night. Plus, footage of roadrunners cruising the spectacular deserts of Arizona, and polar bears leaping from rocks to hunt beluga whales(n)

programme.title: The Blame Game
programme.desc: Comedian Joanne McNally joins the comedy line-up of Jake O'Kane, Colin Murphy, Neil Delamere and guest host Gerry Kelly to poke fun at the people and events which have been making the news at home and across the planet over the past week(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Dwayne Johnson and Kevin Hart talk about their new movie Jumanji: The Next Level, Jodie Whittaker discusses the new season of Doctor Who and Michael Palin reflects on his latest travel book, North Korea Journal. Plus, music by - and a word from - singer-songwriter Harry Styles(n)

programme.title: One Night: Joshua v Ruiz
programme.desc: A look back at the first bout between Anthony Joshua and Andy Ruiz Jr, which took place on June 1 this year. Both fighters, former boxers and fans who were there on the night, including Sylvester Stallone, recount their experiences of the night, which ended in a stunning upset(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: As the tenth week of the competition begins, Lord sugar summons the candidates to Burlington House in Central London. Here, the remaining candidates are informed that for this week's task, they have been challenged to create and sell their own perfume, coming up with their own signature scent and design to sell to major retailers. For one team a failure to make decisions proves problematic, while on the other team a bold attempt to make their fragrance stand out does not go to plan. Back in the boardroom, one of the candidates is sent home from the competition(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Host Matt Tebbutt is joined by fellow chefs Ruth Hansom and Jose Pizarro, while actor and singer Luke Evans faces his food heaven or hell. Olly Smith chooses the wines(n)

programme.title: Flour Power
programme.desc: Lennox, Fraser, Jamie and Becca- colleagues from the combined companies Avian and Blue2 in Broughty Ferry - compete against each other in a series of baking challenges(n)

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests review the week's headlines and look ahead to forthcoming matches, including the Premier League Manchester derby between City and United at the Etihad Stadium(n)

programme.title: Live Snooker: UK Championship
programme.desc: Hazel Irvine presents coverage of the opening semi-final on the 11th day of the prestigious ranking tournament at the Barbican Centre in York, played over the best of 11 frames. At the corresponding stage last year, eventual champion Ronnie O'Sullivan recorded a 6-1 victory over outsider Tom Ford to progress to the final. With commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, John Parrott, Ken Doherty, Stephen Hendry, Alan McManus and Dennis Taylor(n)

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland(n)

programme.title: Final Score from Northern Ireland
programme.desc: A round-up of the day's action along with the classified results(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as famous TV experts take part in the general knowledge quiz, trying to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. Taking part are Mary-Ann Ochota, Adam Hart-Davis, Martin Roberts, Michael Fish, Carrie Grant, David Grant, Charlie Ross and Catherine Southon(n)

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present a music-based quiz show in which contestants must name as many hit songs and artists as possible under intense pressure, with a £10,000 jackpot up for grabs. This week's contestants are couple Alana and Scott from Alexandria in Scotland, brother and sister Devon and Davina from London and Mancunian sisters Sy and Seleena, whose dad used to be a popstar(n)

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman host the semi-final, in which Emma Barton, Kelvin Fletcher, Chris Ramsey and Karim Zeroual find themselves one step closer to hoisting the Glitterball trophy aloft. As the competition is in its final stages, each couple will now perform not one but two routines in a bid to impress the judges and audience at home. Shirley Ballas, Bruno Tonioli, Craig Revel Horwood and Motsi Mabuse will be on hand to give their penultimate judgement(n)

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's Big Show
programme.desc: The lively comedian hosts an evening of entertainment, recorded in front of an audience at the London Palladium. Joining Michael this week are global superstar Camila Cabello, who will be singing her new single Easy, and former King of the Jungle and football legend Harry Redknapp, who hands over his phone to play Send to All. Michael also wakes up one of the biggest boy bands in the world for an extra special Midnight Gameshow with Westlife, plus there are incredible surprises in store for members of the audience(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Charlie learns that his friends are always there for him as he struggles to come to terms with Duffy's advancing illness, and the realisation that he can't afford to pay for her long-term care. Archie saves a hungover Will from partnering Dylan on his shift and the pair get to work on treating a guest patient, while Rash feels isolated and left behind as Mason flies ever higher(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Manchester City v Manchester United at the Etihad Stadium and Everton v Chelsea at Goodison Park. Pep Guardiola's side won both meetings with their fierce rivals last season, including a 3-1 victory on this ground in November 2018, with David Silva, Sergio Aguero and Ilkay Gundogan scoring the goals. Plus, action from Bournemouth v Liverpool at the Vitality Stadium, Tottenham Hotspur v Burnley at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium, and Watford v Crystal Palace at Vicarage Road(n)

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by former players Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present highlights of Thursday night's game - Chicago Bears v Dallas Cowboys at Soldier Field(n)

programme.title: Memento (2000)
programme.desc: An insurance investigator suffers from short-term memory loss after an attack in which his wife was also raped and murdered. Unable to remember events for more than a few minutes, he is determined to track down the killer and relies on copious note-taking and reminders tattooed on his body to aid his mission. Christopher Nolan's thriller, starring Guy Pearce, Carrie-Anne Moss, Joe Pantoliano and Mark Boone Jr(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Chris Mason and Babita Sharma present a round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Manchester City v Manchester United at the Etihad Stadium and Everton v Chelsea at Goodison Park. Pep Guardiola's side won both meetings with their fierce rivals last season, including a 3-1 victory on this ground in November 2018, with David Silva, Sergio Aguero and Ilkay Gundogan scoring the goals. Plus, action from Bournemouth v Liverpool at the Vitality Stadium, Tottenham Hotspur v Burnley at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium, and Watford v Crystal Palace at Vicarage Road(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00(n)

programme.title: Sunday Politics Northern Ireland
programme.desc: The latest political news and debate, and reports from around the Province(n)

programme.title: Fern Britton Meets
programme.desc: The Pointless host discusses how being sent to boarding school helped him develop his singing and his religious beliefs, and reveals an obsession with llamas(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Seeing her daughter cope with cerebral palsy has inspired Bradford mum Nicola that a new life in Perth, Australia is the way forward. Nicki Chapman follows the family's progress(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Thomas Plant guide two teams scouring the Royal Welsh Showground in Builth Wells for bargains to take to auction. Presenter Charlie Ross monitors their progress and also finds out about a weird and wonderful collection of bicycles(n)

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Claire McCollum is at Beamish open-air museum in Co Durham to find out how Edwardians celebrated Christmas. Plus, music for Advent, with Rick Astley performing When a Child Is Born(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Perfect Town
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin introduces a London couple to his home town of Berkhamsted, Hertfordshire, taking them on a trip to the nearby Grand Union Canal and showcasing the local craft ales(n)

programme.title: Madagascar: Escape 2 Africa (2008)
programme.desc: The escaped zoo animals discover a plane and, following a swift repair job by the penguins, manage to escape the desolate shores of Madagascar. However, they crash-land on the plains of Africa and find themselves face-to-face with their wild counterparts for the first time. Animated comedy sequel, with the voices of Ben Stiller, Chris Rock, David Schwimmer and Jada Pinkett Smith(n)

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at life in North America, which is defined by extreme weather and seasonal change - from tornados that roar across the prairies, to arctic air sweeping through the humid, southern swamps. In winter, lynx prowl the snowy Yukon for snowshoe hares, while Florida manatees seek hot springs to escape the freeze, while in summer fireflies light up the forests at night. Plus, footage of roadrunners cruising the spectacular deserts of Arizona, and polar bears leaping from rocks to hunt beluga whales(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team are in Wiltshire where John Craven is in a wood that's ravaged with ash dieback, and Anita Rani meets a craftsman who's keeping the ancient art of bee skep making alive. Steve Brown pays a visit to one of the country's top wood engravers, and Adam Henson is in Lincolnshire seeing for himself the impact of the recent devastating floods(n)

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at the fauna of Africa, home to the greatest wildlife gatherings on Earth. But even in this land of plenty, animals face huge challenges. At its heart is a vast tropical rainforest full of life, where young chimpanzees learn how to use tools to make the most of the jungles riches. With knowledge passed down from generation to generation, they can access the best forest foods(n)

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman present the last results show, which will decide who will be going through to next weekend's live final. The scores awarded by judges Shirley Ballas, Motsi Mabuse, Bruno Tonioli and Craig Revel Horwood are combined with the viewers' votes, and the two lowest scoring couples must compete in a dance-off to retain their place in the contest. With music by Niall Horan and a performance by ballet dancer Carlos Acosta(n)

programme.title: His Dark Materials
programme.desc: Lyra discovers the horrific truth behind the Gobblers' activities in the North. She must now use all her wits to help free those around her and avoid suffering a terrible fate. Adaptation of Philip Pullman's fantasy adventure novel series, starring Dafne Keen(n)

programme.title: Elizabeth Is Missing
programme.desc: When 80-year-old Maud's best friend Elizabeth fails to turn up for a shopping trip, she is certain that something terrible has happened. Despite living with dementia, she decides to turn detective and sets about trying to solve the mystery, but with her condition it's not always easy to hold on to the clues. Drama, starring Glenda Jackson(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by studio guests to review the day's Premier League action, featuring Aston Villa v Leicester City at Villa Park, Newcastle United v Southampton at St James' Park, Norwich City v Sheffield United at Carrow Road and Brighton & Hove Albion v Wolverhampton Wanderers at Amex Stadium. Plus, all the goals from yesterday's top-flight matches(n)

programme.desc: The TV version of the popular radio show taking a light-hearted view of all things related to Brexit and beyond, with Laura Kuenssberg, Katya Adler, Adam Fleming and Chris Mason engaging in banter about the ongoing process to leave the EU(n)

programme.desc: The leaders of the two main political parties - Boris Johnson of the Conservatives and Jeremy Corbyn of Labour - go head to head in a forum chaired in Southampton by Nick Robinson. programme.desc: A gathering at the Devon estate of Miss Marple's old school friend Lady Tressilian leads to murder when the eccentric Mr Treves tells the story of an unconvicted child killer.
